name: Gottschalk-Gleser Content Analysis Scales
authors: Louis A. Gottschalk & Goldine C. Gleser
source: Manual of Instructions for Using the Gottschalk-Gleser Content Analysis Scales (UC Press)
standard_sample: 5 minutes of free speech to ambiguous prompt

scales.name: Anxiety
scales.description: Total anxiety is the sum of all weighted scores across all six subtypes

subtypes.name: Death Anxiety
subtypes.description: References to death, dying, threat of death, or anxiety about death

categories.description: Self dying, being dead, threat of death to self
Others dying, being dead, threat of death to others
Inanimate objects destroyed, ceasing to exist
Denial of death anxiety

subtypes.name: Mutilation (Castration) Anxiety
subtypes.description: References to injury, tissue damage, physical harm, illness, body damage, or anxiety about such

categories.description: Self injured, ill, body damaged
Others injured, ill, body damaged
Inanimate objects damaged, broken
Denial of mutilation anxiety

subtypes.name: Separation Anxiety
subtypes.description: References to desertion, abandonment, loneliness, ostracism, loss of support, falling, loss of love or love object

categories.description: Self abandoned, deserted, lonely, lost
Others abandoned, deserted, lonely
Inanimate objects lost, missing, falling
Denial of separation anxiety

subtypes.name: Guilt Anxiety
subtypes.description: References to adverse criticism, abuse, condemnation, moral disapproval, guilt, or threat of such

categories.description: Self criticized, condemned, morally disapproved of
Others criticized, condemned, morally disapproved of
Denial of guilt anxiety

subtypes.name: Shame Anxiety
subtypes.description: References to ridicule, inadequacy, shame, embarrassment, humiliation, overexposure of deficiencies or private details

categories.description: Self ridiculed, shamed, embarrassed, exposed
Others ridiculed, shamed, embarrassed
Denial of shame anxiety

subtypes.name: Diffuse (Nonspecific) Anxiety
subtypes.description: Nonspecific or generalized anxiety references that do not fit subtypes 1-5

categories.description: Self experiencing nonspecific anxiety, dread, unease
Others experiencing nonspecific anxiety
Denial of diffuse anxiety

scales.name: Hostility Directed Outward
scales.description: Destructive, injurious, critical thoughts and actions directed to others. Divided into overt and covert.

categories.description: Self killing, fighting, injuring other individuals, or threatening to do so
Self robbing, abandoning others, causing suffering or anguish, or threatening to do so
Self adversely criticizing, depreciating, blaming, expressing anger, dislike of other human beings
Self harming domestic animals/pets, or threatening to do so
Self abandoning or robbing animals, or threatening to do so
Mild or vague criticism/depreciation of others
Depriving or disappointing others
Others dying or being killed violently, or being threatened with such
Bodies (human or domestic animals) mutilated, depreciated, defiled
Hostile actions toward wildlife, flora, or inanimate objects, or threats
Criticism directed at subhuman, inanimate objects, places, situations
Using hostile words, cursing, mention of anger or rage without referent
Injury or death from non-human sources (natural disasters, animals, etc.)
Subhuman entities engaged in aggression toward each other
Denial of anger, dislike, hatred, cruelty, and intent to harm

composites.overt_hostility: Categories where the speaker is the agent: a3, b3, c3, a2, b2, c2, d2, a1
composites.covert_hostility: Categories where others or situations are agents: e2, f2, d1, e1, b1, c1

categories.description: Self attempting or threatening to kill self, with or without conscious intent
Self wanting to die, needing or deserving to die
Self-injury or disfigurement, or threats of such
Self-blame, self-hatred, feeling worthless
Hopelessness, despair, depression, lack of purpose
Beliefs about deserving punishment or needing atonement
Self-criticism, regret, shame, or acknowledging personal errors
Feelings of deprivation, disappointment, loneliness
Disappointment in oneself; unmet personal expectations
Denial of anger or destructive impulses toward self
Feeling pressured to meet personal standards

scales.name: Ambivalent Hostility
scales.description: Hostile threats and actions directed toward the self from external sources — neither clearly outward nor inward

categories.description: Others (human) killing or threatening to kill self
Others (human) physically injuring, mutilating, disfiguring self, or threatening to do so
Others (human) adversely criticizing, blaming, expressing anger or dislike toward self
Others (human) abandoning, robbing self, causing suffering, anguish, or threatening to do so
Others (human) depriving, disappointing, misunderstanding self, or threatening to do so
Self threatened with death from subhuman or inanimate object, or death-dealing situation
Others (subhuman, inanimate, or situation) injuring, abandoning, robbing self, causing suffering
Denial of blame

scales.name: Hope
scales.description: Seven categories, positive scores indicate hope, negative indicate hopelessness

categories.description: References to self or others getting or receiving help, advice, support, sustenance, confidence, esteem from others or self
References to feelings of optimism about the present or future
References to being, wanting to be, or seeking to be the recipient of good fortune, good luck, God's favor or blessing
References to hopes that lead to constructive outcome, survival, longevity, smooth interpersonal relationships (requires explicit use of 'hope', 'wish', or close synonym)
References to NOT being, wanting, or seeking good fortune, luck, favor, or blessing
References to self or others NOT getting or receiving help, advice, support, sustenance, confidence, esteem
References to feelings of hopelessness, losing hope, despair, lack of confidence, lack of ambition, pessimism, discouragement

scales.name: Social Alienation-Personal Disorganization
scales.description: Originally devised as a 'schizophrenic' scale. Positive weights indicate pathology, negative indicate healthy functioning.

items.description: Bad, dangerous, low value, strange, ill
Word repetition separated by one word

scales.name: Cognitive and Intellectual Impairment
scales.description: Measures both transient and permanent cognitive changes, principally due to brain dysfunction

key_categories.description: Denial of feelings, attitudes, mental state

key_principle: Statements about others experiencing anxiety or danger are treated as cues to the speaker's own anxieties. Spontaneous denials also indicate probable low-level presence of the affect.
